title: System, method, and non-transitory storage media for a quick-bid of an online wager transaction
abstract: A system, method and computer product to determine a fair price for an online exchange of all or part of a wager or fantasy sports entry. A user accesses the exchange application. Information associated with a ticket and selected for purchase by the user is displayed. The user indicates that the user wishes to bid by way of a quick-bid option. The user submits a first-counter offer. The seller rejects the first counter-offer and submits a second counter-offer. The user rejects the second counter offer and submits a third counter-offer. The seller accepts the third counter-offer. A transaction between the user and the seller is processed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a database stored on a server; an exchange application associated with a wagering service and installed on a user device accessible to a user, wherein the user device includes a user interface; and a processing device of the server, wherein the processing device is in communication with the user interface, the processing device including: a hosting unit configured to prompt a user to access the exchange application, and display ticket information associated with a ticket selected for purchase by the user from a seller, a communications unit configured to receive an indication from the user that the user wishes to bid by way of a quick-bid option, prompt the user to submit a first-counter offer, and receive the first counter-offer from the user, the hosting unit further configured to prompt the seller to accept or reject the first counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the seller rejects the first counter-offer and configured to receive a second counter-offer, the hosting unit further configured to present the second counter-offer to the user and prompt the seller to accept or reject the second counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the user rejects the second counter-offer and configured to receive a third counter-offer, 

 the hosting unit further configured to prompt the seller to accept or reject the third counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the seller accepts the third counter-offer, and a ticket management unit configured to process a transaction between the user and the seller. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the first counter-offer is at least 60% of a listed firm bid price. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the second counteroffer is higher than the first counter-offer. 4. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the transaction includes: deducting, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer from an account associated with the user. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, wherein the transaction further includes: depositing, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer to an account associated with the seller. 6. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: transferring, by the ticket management unit, the ticket from an account associated with the seller to an account associated with the user. 7. A computer-implemented method comprising: prompting a user to access, by a hosting unit, an exchange website associated with a wagering service; displaying, by the hosting unit, ticket information associated with a ticket 

 selected for purchase by the user from a seller; receiving, by a communications unit, an indication from the user that the user wishes to bid by way of a quick-bid option; prompting the user, by the communications unit, to submit a first-counter offer; receiving, by the communications unit, the first counter-offer from the user; prompting the seller, by the hosting unit, to accept or reject the first counteroffer; receiving, by the communications unit, an indication that the seller rejects the first counter-offer and configured to receive a second counter-offer; presenting, by the hosting unit, the second counter-offer to the user; prompting the seller, by the hosting unit, to accept or reject the second counter-offer; receiving, by the communications unit, an indication that the user rejects the second counter-offer and configured to receive a third counter-offer; prompting the seller, by the hosting unit, to accept or reject the third counteroffer; receiving, by the communications unit, an indication that the seller accepts the third counter-offer; and process, by a ticket management unit, a transaction between the user and the seller. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, wherein the first counter-offer is at least 60% of a listed firm bid price. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, wherein the second counteroffer is higher than the first counter-offer. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, wherein the transaction includes: deducting, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer from an account associated with the user. 11. The computer- implemented method of claim 10, wherein the transaction further includes: depositing, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer to an account associated with the seller. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, further comprising: transferring, by the ticket management unit, the ticket from an account associated with the seller to an account associated with the user. 13. A non-transitory information recording medium on which a computer readable program is recorded that causes a computer to function as a system comprising: a database stored on a server; an exchange application associated with a wagering service and installed on a user device accessible to a user, wherein the user device includes a user interface; and a processing device of the server, wherein the processing device is in communication with the user interface, the processing device including: a hosting unit configured to prompt a user to access the exchange application, and display ticket information associated with a ticket selected for purchase by the user from a seller, 

 a communications unit configured to receive an indication from the user that the user wishes to bid by way of a quick-bid option, prompt the user to submit a first-counter offer, and receive the first counter-offer from the user, the hosting unit further configured to prompt the seller to accept or reject the first counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the seller rejects the first counter-offer and configured to receive a second counter-offer, the hosting unit further configured to present the second counter-offer to the user and prompt the seller to accept or reject the second counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the user rejects the second counter-offer and configured to receive a third counter-offer, the hosting unit further configured to prompt the seller to accept or reject the third counter-offer, the communications unit further configured to receive an indication that the seller accepts the third counter-offer, and a ticket management unit configured to process a transaction between the user and the seller. 14. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, wherein the first counter-offer is at least 60% of a listed firm bid price. 15. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, wherein the second counter-offer is higher than the first counter-offer. 16. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, wherein the transaction includes: deducting, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer from an account associated with the user. 17. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 16, wherein the transaction further includes: depositing, by the ticket management unit, an amount equal to the third counter-offer to an account associated with the seller. 18. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, further comprising: transferring, by the ticket management unit, the ticket from an account associated with the seller to an account associated with the user. 19. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, further comprising: triggering, by the ticket management unit, a 15-minute response window after the first counter-offer is placed. 20. The non-transitory information recording medium of claim 13, further comprising: triggering, by the ticket management unit, a 5 -minute response window after the second counter-offer is placed.
